description:
Authors: Wei, J. and Wang, X. and Schuurmans, D. and Bosma, M. and Ichter, B. and Xia, F. and Chi, E. and Le, Q. and Zhou, D.
Title: Chain-of-thought prompting elicits reasoning in large language models
Conference/Book: Advances in Neural Information Processing Systems
Year: 2022
Volume: 35
reason: Paper confirmed by multiple authoritative sources including the official NeurIPS 2022 proceedings and arXiv (arXiv:2201.11903). Title, authors, year, and venue match. Semantic Scholar's failure to return results is likely due to indexing issues, not paper nonexistence.
prompt_variant: You are a critical academic research verifier. Your task: determine if a bibliog...
_search_results:
**Yes, the paper exists.**

**Exact title:** Chain-of-Thought Prompting Elicits Reasoning in Large Language Models[1][2][3][4][5][7][8].

**Authors:** Jason Wei, Xuezhi Wang, Dale Schuurmans, Maarten Bosma, Brian Ichter, Fei Xia, Ed Chi, Quoc Le, Denny Zhou (note: Wei Wang is not listed as an author; the query likely misattributes this)[4][7][8].

**Venue:** 36th Conference on Neural Information Processing Systems (NeurIPS 2022)[1][2][3][8].

**Year:** 2022[1][2][3][4][8].

**DOI/URL:** arXiv preprint at https://arxiv.org/abs/2201.11903; official NeurIPS proceedings page at https://proceedings.neurips.cc/paper/2022/hash/9d5609613524ecf4f15af0f7b31abca4-Abstract-Conference.html[3][4].
Semantic Scholar: no matching paper found.

**Yes, the paper exists.**

- **Exact title**: Chain-of-Thought Prompting Elicits Reasoning in Large Language Models[1][3][5]
- **Authors**: Jason Wei, Xuezhi Wang, Dale Schuurmans, Maarten Bosma, Brian Ichter, Fei Xia, Ed H. Chi, Quoc V. Le, Denny Zhou[1][3][5]
- **Venue**: arXiv (preprint)[3][5]
- **Year**: 2022 (submitted v1 on 28 Jan 2022, last revised 10 Jan 2023 as v6)[5]
- **DOI**: 10.48550/arXiv.2201.11903[3][5]
- **URL**: https://arxiv.org/abs/2201.11903 (abstract); https://arxiv.org/pdf/2201.11903 (PDF)[1][5]
Semantic Scholar: no matching paper found.

The paper **"Chain-of-Thought Prompting Elicits Reasoning in Large Language Models"** exists and was published at NeurIPS 2022[1][2].

**Key Details:**

- **Authors:** Jason Wei, Xuezhi Wang, Dale Schuurmans, Maarten Bosma, Brian Ichter, Fei Xia, Ed Chi, Quoc Le, Denny Zhou[3]
- **Venue:** 36th Conference on Neural Information Processing Systems (NeurIPS 2022)[1]
- **Conference Dates:** November 28 - December 9, 2022, New Orleans, Louisiana[6]
- **Pages:** 24824-24837 (14 pages)[6]
- **DOI/URLs:** 
  - arXiv: https://arxiv.org/abs/2201.11903[3]
  - NeurIPS Proceedings: https://proceedings.neurips.cc/paper/2022/hash/9d5609613524ecf4f15af0f7b31abca4-Abstract-Conference.html[2]
  - PDF: https://webdocs.cs.ualberta.ca/~daes/papers/neurips22a.pdf[1]

**Summary:** The paper demonstrates that **chain-of-thought prompting**—providing intermediate reasoning steps in prompts—significantly improves large language models' performance on arithmetic, commonsense, and symbolic reasoning tasks[2]. For example, a 540B-parameter model with just eight chain-of-thought exemplars achieved state-of-the-art accuracy on the GSM8K benchmark of math word problems[2].
Semantic Scholar: no matching paper found.
